recipes.title: Aged Eggnog

ingredients.name: freshly grated nutmeg, plus more for garnish

steps: Separate the eggs and store the whites for another application.
Beat the yolks with the sugar and nutmeg in a large mixing bowl until the mixture lightens in color and falls off the whisk in a solid ribbon.
Combine dairy (half-and-half, milk, cream), booze (rum, cognac, bourbon), and salt in a second bowl or pitcher.
Slowly beat the dairy-booze mixture into the egg mixture until fully combined.
Transfer to a large glass jar (or a couple of smaller ones) and store in the refrigerator.
Age for a minimum of 2 months for best results.
Serve in mugs or cups topped with freshly grated nutmeg.
notes: The alcohol 'cooks' the eggs over time, making this safe to drink despite raw eggs
Can be aged for 2 weeks minimum, but 2 months is preferred - a year is possible if you can wait
Use pasteurized eggs if you want extra peace of mind
The mix of three spirits (rum, cognac, bourbon) adds depth and complexity
Save the egg whites for meringue, angel food cake, or cocktails
Descends from English sack posset, a thick beverage made with eggs, milk, and fortified wine

recipes.title: Baked Mac and Cheese
steps: Preheat oven to 350Â°F (175Â°C). Butter a large baking dish.
Shred all cheeses (cheddar, provolone, gruyere) and keep separate.
Cook elbow macaroni one minute less than al dente according to package directions. Drain.
Drizzle the macaroni with olive oil and stir to coat. Set aside to cool.
Make panko topping: Mix panko crumbs, parmesan, melted butter (56g), and paprika in a small bowl. Set aside.
Combine shredded cheddar, provolone, and gruyere in a bowl and set aside.
Melt both butters (42g unsalted + 42g salted) together in a large saucepan.
Whisk in the flour over low to medium heat and cook until it bubbles and turns lightly golden.
Gradually whisk in the milk and heavy cream until smooth. Keep whisking until the mixture begins to bubble.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add two cups of the shredded cheese mixture and whisk until smooth.
Add the rest of the shredded cheese and whisk until the sauce becomes very creamy.
Stir in the cooled macaroni until everything is well coated.
Pour half of the mac and cheese into the prepared baking dish.
Sprinkle part of the panko topping over the first layer.
Add the rest of the mac and cheese.
Sprinkle the remaining panko topping over the top.
Bake at 350Â°F for about 30 minutes, or until bubbly and golden brown.
Serve immediately.
notes: Volume to weight conversions are approximate: 2 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 28g, 4 heaping tbsp flour â‰ˆ 40g, 1.5 cups panko â‰ˆ 90g, 4 tbsp butter â‰ˆ 56g
Total butter used: 84g for sauce + 56g for topping = 140g
For best results, shred cheese fresh rather than using pre-shredded

recipes.title: Banana Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 350Â°F (175Â°C). Coat an 8Ã—4 inch loaf pan with cooking spray or butter.
Place peeled bananas in a microwave-safe bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and cut a few slits for air vents.
Microwave on high for 4-5 minutes.
Pour bananas into a strainer over a bowl. Let liquid drain for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally (should yield about Â½ cup liquid).
Transfer banana liquid to a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Boil, stirring often, until reduced by half (about Â¼ cup).
Transfer mashed bananas to a large mixing bowl. Pour reduced liquid over bananas and stir until fairly smooth.
Whisk in brown sugar, eggs, cooled melted butter, and vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
Gradually add flour mixture to banana mixture until just combined.
Fold in pecans if desired.
Pour into prepared loaf pan. Sprinkle turbinado sugar evenly over batter.
Bake 50-60 minutes until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool in pan for 15 minutes on a wire rack. Remove from pan and cool completely.
notes: Volume conversions: 5 large bananas â‰ˆ 625g, 8 tbsp butter â‰ˆ 113g, 2 eggs â‰ˆ 100g, Â¾ cup brown sugar â‰ˆ 150g, 1 tsp vanilla â‰ˆ 5g, 1Â¾ cups flour â‰ˆ 210g, 1 tsp baking soda â‰ˆ 5g, Â½ tsp salt â‰ˆ 3g, Â½ tsp cinnamon â‰ˆ 3g, Â½ cup pecans â‰ˆ 60g, 1 tbsp turbinado sugar â‰ˆ 12g
Recipe adapted from America's Test Kitchen
Microwaving and reducing banana liquid intensifies banana flavor

recipes.title: Banh Flan (Vietnamese CrÃ¨me Caramel)
steps: Make caramel: Heat sugar, water, and lemon juice in a small saucepan over medium heat. Meanwhile, prepare a large bowl of iced water.
Once caramel turns amber (about 3-5 minutes), remove from heat and place saucepan into iced water for 10 seconds. Be careful not to splash water into caramel.
Pour or spoon caramel into 6 molds (about 150ml each). Leave to harden.
Make custard: Whisk eggs in a medium bowl and set aside.
Put milk, sugar, vanilla, and salt in medium saucepan over medium heat. Stir until sugar dissolves.
Continue heating until mixture reaches 70Â°C (158Â°F).
Preheat oven to 150Â°C (300Â°F).
Remove milk from heat. Slowly add eggs through a strainer while mixing.
Strain mixture again, then pour into molds over the hardened caramel.
Place a tea towel on workbench. Tap molds on towel a few times to remove air bubbles.
Cover each mold with aluminum foil. Poke a few holes through the foil.
Place a tea towel in bottom of baking tray. Put molds in tray.
Fill tray halfway up the molds with medium-hot tap water (not boiling).
Bake for 35 minutes.
Remove molds from tray and cool for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Carefully remove foil. Let cool until cool enough to handle.
Cover each mold with cling wrap. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours (preferably 1-2 days for best results).
To serve, run a knife around edges and turn upside down on a plate. Caramel will spill over custard.
notes: Volume conversions: Â¾ cup sugar â‰ˆ 150g, 3 tbsp water â‰ˆ 45g, 1 tsp lemon juice â‰ˆ 5g, 5 eggs â‰ˆ 250g, 600ml milk â‰ˆ 600g, 1 tsp vanilla â‰ˆ 5g, 1 pinch salt â‰ˆ 1g
Requires 6 molds of roughly 150ml each
Chilled and set after 4 hours, but caramel may not be fully liquefied - 1-2 days gives best liquid caramel
Smooth, soft custard with slightly bitter dark amber caramel that balances sweetness
Vietnamese and French fusion dessert

recipes.title: Billionaire Brownie

ingredients.name: large eggs, tempered to room temperature (about 4)
bread flour (or cassava flour for gluten-free)
sweetened condensed milk (for dulce de leche)

steps: MAKE DULCE DE LECHE: Heat sous vide bath to 185Â°F (85Â°C). Submerge unopened can of sweetened condensed milk and cook for 4 hours. Let cool before opening.
PREP: Preheat oven to 350Â°F (177Â°C) with rack in center for at least 30 minutes. Line a 9x13 inch metal pan with parchment paper, extending up the short sides. Spray with non-stick spray.
Sift flour and cocoa powder together twice. Set aside.

MELT CHOCOLATE: Set up a bain marie (bowl over simmering water at 160-170Â°F). Add chopped chocolates and diced butter. Stir occasionally until fully melted, about 10 minutes. Keep warm over the water.
MAKE ITALIAN MERINGUE: Add sugar to a small pan, pour water around the sides. Insert probe thermometer. Cook over medium heat to 248Â°F (120Â°C) hardball stage.
When syrup is 10Â°F from target, start whipping eggs on high speed until doubled and pale yellow.
When syrup reaches 248Â°F, remove from heat. With mixer running, slowly pour syrup in a thin stream into the eggs (avoid the sides of the bowl).
Whip for 15 minutes until cooled slightly and meringue has structure.
COMBINE: Turn mixer to low. Slowly drizzle in the warm melted chocolate. Scrape bowl and mix until incorporated.
Add vanilla, kosher salt, and miso paste. Mix for 1 minute.
Add the sifted flour-cocoa mixture. Mix on low for 2 minutes, scrape bowl, mix another minute until homogenous.
ASSEMBLE: Pour 1/3 of batter into prepared pan, smooth out. Drizzle 1/3 of dulce de leche in stripes and swirl with a skewer. Sprinkle chocolate chunks over top.
Pour remaining batter on top. Drizzle more dulce de leche and swirl. Finish with Maldon flake salt.
BAKE: Bake 48-50 minutes until internal temp is 195-200Â°F (91-93Â°C), or until toothpick comes out with moist crumbs (not wet). The center will dome then collapse when done.
Cool for at least 2 hours before slicing. Use parchment edges to lift brownies from pan.
notes: Called Billionaire Brownie because it's obscenely rich.
White miso adds a mellow umami that balances the sweetness - don't skip it.
The Italian meringue technique creates the dense fudgy interior and glassy crackly top.
Dulce de leche: sous vide at 185Â°F - 4 hours for caramel, up to 12 hours for darker/thicker.
For gluten-free: substitute bread flour with Otto's Cassava Flour (brand matters).
Use a metal pan for crispy edges. Glass or ceramic pans need 10-15 extra minutes.
Keep melted chocolate above 100Â°F or it will thicken and ruin the glassy top.

recipes.title: Blue Cheese Foam Sequence
steps: For Blue Cheese Foam: Mix blue cheese, milk, and olive oil (reserving heavy cream) in a blender. Blend until smooth.
Pass mixture through a fine sieve.

Add heavy cream, season to taste with salt and pepper and stir. Keep in mind that the foam is going to expand 2 or 3 times in the ISI Whip so you need to season 2 or 3 times more than what you would normally do.
Pour mixture into ISI Whip, screw 1 cream charger (2 if using 1L ISI Whip) and shake vigorously after each one.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
For Port Jelly: In a saucepan heat the port with Agar Agar over medium heat while you continuously whisk.
Let it simmer while whisking for 5 minutes to reduce the port a little and intensify the flavor.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and pass the mixture through a fine sieve to remove any bubbles.
Pour the Port and Agar mixture into a flat container to form a layer of 7mm (0.28 in) thick. Let it cool at room temperature on a flat surface.

Once the Port is jellified, cut it in 1 cm (0.4 in) squares using a knife. Use a small spatula to handle the squares. If you are not going to use them right away, store them in a sealed container in the fridge.
To Assemble: Get your Anti-Griddle ready (freeze a metal baking sheet or use dry ice).
For each serving, pump 5 dollops of blue cheese foam onto the Anti-Griddle. The dollop should be about 1.9 to 2.5 cm (3/4 to 1 inch) in diameter and 1.3 cm (1/2 inch) high.
Place the pairing ingredients on top of each dollop: walnut, celery, grape, pear and port jelly square.

If using a home-made Anti-Griddle, the total time the dollop should be on the cold surface is between 1Â½-2 minutes. The bottom should be frozen hard but the center and top of the dollop should be soft and at room temperature.
Transfer dollop to serving plate using a spatula. Serving plate should be cold so it gives you time to prepare all plates before serving.
notes: Volume conversions: 100g blue cheese â‰ˆ 3.5 oz, 125ml heavy cream â‰ˆ 125g, 70ml milk â‰ˆ 70g, 40ml olive oil â‰ˆ 40g, 140g port, 1g agar agar â‰ˆ 0.7%, 4 walnuts â‰ˆ 20g, 4 celery slices â‰ˆ 20g, 4 pear slices â‰ˆ 20g, 4 grape halves â‰ˆ 20g
Requires special equipment: ISI Whip with cream chargers, Anti-Griddle or freezer-safe metal sheet
An Anti-Griddle is a molecular gastronomy equipment made by PolyScience - you can make a DIY version at home using a frozen metal baking sheet
Blue cheese foam pairs with walnuts, celery, port, pears and grapes - stimulates the senses with a variety of flavor pairings, temperatures and textures
For an extra twist, carbonate the grapes to make them fizzy and resemble champagne

recipes.title: Chinese Almond Cookies
steps: Place almond flour, salt, and chilled butter cubes into an electric mixer with paddle attachment. Beat on medium speed for 3 minutes until coarse and chunky.
Add 1 egg (50g) and almond extract. Mix on low speed until just incorporated.
Sift together all-purpose flour, sugar, and baking soda. Add to mixture and mix on low speed until just combined.
Flatten dough into a disc, wrap in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 325Â°F (165Â°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Beat the remaining egg (50g) in a small bowl.
Roll dough into balls about Â¾ inch wide. Place on baking sheet about 1 inch apart.
Press down slightly with your palm to make a coin shape.
Press one sliced almond into the center of each cookie.
Using a pastry brush or finger, paint each cookie with beaten egg for a lacquered appearance.
Bake at 325Â°F for 13-15 minutes until edges just begin to tan.
Cool on the sheet on a wire rack.
notes: Volume conversions: 1â…“ cups almond flour â‰ˆ 130g, 1 cup butter (2 sticks) = 227g, 2 large eggs â‰ˆ 100g, 1 tsp almond extract â‰ˆ 5g, 1Â¾ cups flour â‰ˆ 210g, 1 cup + 2 tbsp sugar â‰ˆ 225g, Â½ tsp baking soda â‰ˆ 3g
Cook time is per batch - baking all 60 cookies will take about 1 hour if doing one sheet at a time
Dough must chill for 2 hours before baking
Egg wash gives cookies a shiny, lacquered finish

recipes.title: Cilbir (Turkish Poached Eggs)

ingredients.name: Aleppo pepper or red pepper flakes

steps: Combine room-temperature yogurt, minced garlic, and kosher salt in a bowl. Whisk well.
Divide yogurt mixture between two serving bowls and set aside.
Bring a medium saucepan of water to a boil and stir in vinegar.
Crack an egg into a fine mesh sieve over a bowl. Gently swirl to drain excess egg white. Transfer to a ramekin.
Create a vortex in boiling water using a wooden spoon. Add egg to center and cook 2-3 minutes.
Remove egg with slotted spoon to parchment-lined plate.
Repeat with second egg.
While second egg cooks, warm olive oil and Aleppo pepper in a small skillet over medium heat.
Place poached eggs on yogurt mixture.
Drizzle with heated spiced oil.
Serve immediately with rustic bread.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 cup yogurt â‰ˆ 245g, 1-2 garlic cloves â‰ˆ 6g, 3 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 42g, 1-2 tbsp vinegar â‰ˆ 15g, 2 tsp Aleppo pepper â‰ˆ 6g
Yogurt should be at room temperature for best results
Traditional Turkish breakfast dish
Serve with crusty bread for dipping

recipes.title: Drunken Shrimp
steps: Rinse the shrimp under running water.
Put shrimp in a deep bowl and pour in rice wine to cover.
Immediately cover the bowl (shrimp will be hopping around if live).
Allow shrimp to drink wine for 15 minutes (or until they stop moving).
Put a medium pot of water to boil. Add ginger slices.
Once boiling, add all shrimp at once and stir a couple times.
When water reboils, cook for 1Â½ minutes more.
Immediately use strainer to scoop shrimp out of water onto your plate.
Serve immediately with soy sauce for dipping if desired.
notes: Volume conversions: 250ml rice wine â‰ˆ 250g, 2 ginger slices â‰ˆ 10g
Recipe calls for 20 fresh shrimp
Live shrimp preferred for traditional preparation
Shrimp is cooked very briefly to maintain tender texture
Traditional Chinese dish where shrimp are intoxicated with wine before cooking

recipes.title: Filipino Chicken Adobo
steps: Combine chicken, soy sauce, and garlic in a bowl. Marinate for at least 1 hour (longer is better).
Heat a pot and add cooking oil.
Pan-fry the marinated chicken 2 minutes per side.
Pour in remaining marinade with garlic and add water. Bring to boil.
Add bay leaves and peppercorns. Simmer 30 minutes until chicken is tender.
Add vinegar, stir, and cook 10 minutes.
Add sugar and salt. Stir and remove from heat.
Serve hot with rice.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 lbs chicken â‰ˆ 907g, 4 tbsp soy sauce â‰ˆ 60g, 6 tbsp vinegar â‰ˆ 90g, 5 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 25g, 1Â½ cups water â‰ˆ 355g, 3 tbsp oil â‰ˆ 42g, 1 tsp sugar â‰ˆ 5g, Â¼ tsp salt â‰ˆ 2g, 1 tsp peppercorns â‰ˆ 3g
Marinating longer enhances flavor significantly
Best served with warm white rice

recipes.title: Cheesy Ground Beef Casserole
steps: Preheat oven to 400Â°F (205Â°C). Lightly spray a 2-quart casserole dish with olive oil.
Heat a large 12-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Lightly spray with olive oil.
Add ground beef and cook, breaking into small pieces, until no longer raw (about 5 minutes). Drain and return to skillet.
Reduce heat to medium. Mix in salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and cayenne pepper.
Slowly mix in cream cheese until fully melted and incorporated.
Turn off heat and stir in half of the shredded cheddar (57g).
Transfer mixture to prepared casserole dish. Sprinkle remaining cheddar on top.
Bake until cheese melts (about 10 minutes).
Garnish with parsley and serve.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 lbs = 907g, 4 oz cream cheese = 113g, 1 cup shredded cheese = 113g, 2 tbsp parsley â‰ˆ 8g
Recipe is keto-friendly and low-carb at 2g carbs per serving

recipes.title: Gyeran Mari (Korean Rolled Omelette)
steps: Beat eggs in a bowl. Add scallions, carrot, and salt. Mix well.
Preheat a small skillet over medium-low heat until it feels hot when you place your hand 2-3 inches above the bottom.
Lightly oil the pan.
Pour a thin layer of egg mixture into the pan (about â…“ to Â½ of total mixture for 3 eggs).
When egg is set but still wet on top, use two spatulas or chopsticks to fold the egg from one side.
Continue rolling the egg toward the opposite side of the pan.
Pull the rolled egg to one side of the pan.
Add a little more oil to the empty space, then pour another portion of egg mixture.
Lift the rolled egg to let the new egg mixture flow underneath.
When the new layer is set but still wet, continue rolling from the existing roll.
Repeat until all egg mixture is used (2-4 pours total depending on egg quantity).
Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Slice into rounds and serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Volume conversions: 3 eggs â‰ˆ 150g, scallions â‰ˆ 20g, carrot â‰ˆ 30g
For 5 eggs, use 3-4 pours. For 3 eggs, use 2-3 pours
Cook over gentle heat - too high and eggs will be tough and break when folded
Use a small skillet for thicker rolls
Popular as lunch box item and street food in Korea
Can add mushrooms, zucchini, bell peppers, garlic chives, ham, bacon, cheese, or gim (seaweed)

recipes.title: Homemade Salsa

ingredients.name: jalapeÃ±o pepper, seeded and roughly chopped

steps: Combine all ingredients in a food processor.
Pulse in 1-second bursts until all ingredients are finely chopped.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed.
Serve with tortilla chips.
For best flavor, refrigerate for a few hours before serving to let flavors marinate together.
notes: Volume conversions: 1Â¼ lbs Roma tomatoes â‰ˆ 567g (about 5-6 tomatoes), 14.5 oz can â‰ˆ 411g, 2 green onions â‰ˆ 30g, â…“ cup red onion â‰ˆ 50g, 1 jalapeÃ±o â‰ˆ 15g, â…“ cup cilantro â‰ˆ 20g, 1 large garlic clove â‰ˆ 5g, 2 tbsp lime juice â‰ˆ 30g, Â½ tsp chili powder â‰ˆ 2g, Â¼ tsp cumin â‰ˆ 2g, Â½ tsp sugar â‰ˆ 2g
Makes about 4.5 cups
Use best quality tomatoes for best flavor
Can substitute canned diced tomatoes with green chilis or fire-roasted tomatoes
Leave jalapeÃ±o seeds for spicier salsa, or use serrano pepper
Can add Â½ bell pepper, poblano, or Anaheim pepper
Add tomato juice or sauce for thinner consistency
Store in refrigerator up to 1 week
Salsa always tastes best after refrigeration

recipes.title: Ice Cream - Build Your Own

options.name: Standard Ice Cream Base
steps: Combine the sugar, dry milk powder, and xanthan gum in a small bowl and stir well.
Pour the corn syrup into a medium pot and stir in the whole milk.
Add the sugar mixture and immediately whisk vigorously until smooth.
Set the pot over medium heat and cook, stirring often and adjusting the heat if necessary to prevent a simmer, until the sugar has fully dissolved, about 3 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat.
Add the cream and whisk until fully combined.
Transfer the mixture to an airtight container and refrigerate until well chilled, at least 6 hours, or for even better texture and flavor, 24 hours.
Stir the base back together if it separates during the resting time. The base can be stored in the fridge for up to 1 week or in the freezer for up to 3 months.

options.name: Blood Pudding

ingredients.name: unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Guittard's Cocoa Rouge)

steps: In a small saucepan, combine the cream, blood, brandy, sugar, cocoa powder, cinnamon stick, salt, coriander seeds, and peppercorns. Thoroughly whisk the ingredients together.
Set the pan over medium-low heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the blood mixture thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 8 minutes.
Toward the end, use an instant-read thermometer to ensure the mixture registers 160Â°F (71Â°C).
Remove the pan from the heat and let the mixture sit for 10 to 15 minutes to cool slightly and steep the spices.
Pour through a fine-mesh strainer into a container and discard the peppercorns, coriander seeds, and cinnamon stick.
Chill until cold and the mixture has a pudding-like consistency, about 3 hours. Use right away or store in the fridge with plastic wrap directly on the surface for up to 2 days.

options.name: Lavender Honey

ingredients.name: natural purple food coloring (optional, preferably India Tree brand)

steps: In a small saucepan, combine the water and honey. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring occasionally, then take it off the heat.
Stir in the lavender, cover the saucepan, and let steep at room temperature for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Pour the syrup through a fine-mesh strainer into a container, pressing on the flower buds to extract as much liquid as possible.
Chill until cold and use right away, or refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

options.name: Goat Cheese with Marionberry-Habanero Jam

ingredients.name: fresh goat cheese (chevre), at room temperature
seedless marionberry, blackberry, or black raspberry jam
fresh habanero chile, coarsely chopped (seeds removed for less heat)

steps: MAKE THE JAM: Combine Â¼ cup (about 80g) of the jam and the habanero in a small saucepan.
Cook over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until the jam liquefies and just begins to simmer, about 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a bowl, then add the remaining jam and stir well to combine.
Refrigerate until fully chilled. The jam keeps in the fridge for up to 6 months.
MAKE THE BASE: Combine the ice cream base, goat cheese, and salt in a bowl and use a stick blender to completely incorporate the cheese.
Stir in the lemon juice just before churning.

ingredients.name: ice cream base, very cold (from above)

steps: Put the ice cream base and the flavor mixture into a bowl and use a stick blender to briefly blend to combine well.
Pour the mixture into an ice cream maker and turn on the machine. Churn just until the mixture has the texture of soft-serve.
Transfer the ice cream into freezer-friendly containers. For flavors with jam/swirl, alternate spooning layers of ice cream and generous dollops of jam.
Cover with parchment paper, pressing it to the surface of the ice cream so it adheres, then cover with a lid.
Store in the coldest part of your freezer (farthest from the door) until firm, at least 6 hours.
Ice cream will keep for up to 3 months.

notes: The base recipe yields a few more tablespoons than needed - this is intentional so you have enough even when some gets left behind in the pan or container.

DRY ICE ALTERNATIVE: Instead of an ice cream maker, use a KitchenAid mixer with paddle attachment. Drop pea-sized pieces of dry ice into the mixture while mixing on low until it reaches soft-serve consistency. Use gloves and work in a ventilated area.
For blood pudding: The flavor is a heady mix of spices, cocoa, and cream cooked into real blood pudding. It carefully toes the line between delectable and frightful.
Pig's blood can often be found frozen at Asian grocery stores or ordered from a butcher.
For lavender: Use culinary-grade dried lavender (pesticide-free) from the spice shop or a specialty store.
For goat cheese: Any fresh chevre will work. The amount of habanero is up to you - more heat makes the next lick of cold cream taste sweeter.
Always fully thaw frozen base before using.
For best texture, chill the base for 24 hours before churning.

recipes.title: Imam Bayildi (Stuffed Eggplants)
steps: Preheat oven to 150Â°C (300Â°F).
Halve the eggplants lengthwise. Score flesh in a crosshatch pattern without breaking the skin.
Rub salt generously into flesh and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large non-stick pan over high heat. Fry eggplant halves in batches until golden.
Place eggplants cut-side up in a baking dish.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion to the pan with remaining oil and salt. Cook 5-6 minutes until soft and lightly colored.
Add garlic and cook 2 minutes until aromatic.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes until breaking down.
Stir in cumin, paprika, oregano, tomato puree, and fresh herbs. Season with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat and stir in 4 tablespoons (60ml) water.
Spoon tomato mixture over eggplant halves. Drizzle remaining oil over dish.
Cover with foil and bake 1.5 hours until eggplants are very soft.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 large eggplants â‰ˆ 1000g, 150ml olive oil â‰ˆ 150g, 1 onion â‰ˆ 150g, 4 garlic cloves â‰ˆ 20g, 4 large tomatoes â‰ˆ 500g, 1 tsp spices â‰ˆ 3g each, 1 tbsp tomato puree â‰ˆ 15g, handful herbs â‰ˆ 10g
Traditional Greek dish meaning 'the imam fainted'
Vegan and vegetarian friendly

recipes.title: Indian Lime Pickle

ingredients.name: fresh ginger, peeled and finely chopped

steps: Cut each lime into 8 wedges. Place in a large bowl.
Sprinkle with salt. Cover and set aside in a cool dry place, stirring occasionally, for 2 days.
Heat oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and cook for 30 seconds or until seeds start to pop.
Add garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and chilli powder. Cook, stirring, for 30 seconds or until aromatic.
Stir in the lime mixture, water, sugar, and vinegar. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes or until mixture is thick.
Spoon into sterilized glass jars. Seal and invert jars for 2 minutes.
Set aside for 1 week to develop flavors before using.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tbsp salt â‰ˆ 15g, 2 tbsp oil â‰ˆ 30g, 2 tsp mustard seeds â‰ˆ 6g, 3 garlic cloves â‰ˆ 15g, 2cm ginger â‰ˆ 15g, 2 tsp cumin â‰ˆ 6g, 2 tsp coriander â‰ˆ 6g, Â½ tsp chilli â‰ˆ 2g, Â½ cup water â‰ˆ 125g, Â¾ cup brown sugar â‰ˆ 125g, 2 tbsp vinegar â‰ˆ 30g
Total time includes 2 days standing and 1 week infusing (about 9 days total)
Choose heavy limes for juiciest results
Store for up to 6 months sealed; once opened, refrigerate for up to 2 months
Serve with Indian curries
Limes are excellent source of vitamin C

recipes.title: JalapeÃ±o Cheddar Cornbread
steps: Preheat oven to 400Â°F (205Â°C) on regular bake setting with rack centered. Lightly grease a 10-inch cast-iron skillet.
Place diced jalapeÃ±os in the hot skillet for 5 minutes. Remove and cool in a bowl. Return skillet to oven to preheat.
Whisk together flour, cornmeal, baking powder, and salt in a small bowl. Set aside.
In a 2-cup measuring cup, combine cooked corn with cream or milk until reaching the 1Â½ cup mark (355ml total).
In a medium bowl, lightly beat eggs. Add the corn-cream mixture and cheddar cheese. Stir together.
In a large bowl, mix butter and sugar until butter absorbs the sugar.
Add egg mixture and combine. Stir in cooled jalapeÃ±os.
Fold in flour mixture gently until barely incorporated.
Remove skillet from oven and coat with nonstick spray. Spoon batter into hot pan.
Top with additional grated cheddar.
Bake 25-28 minutes until golden brown and springy to touch.
Cool in pan at least 30 minutes before serving.
notes: Volume conversions: 1Â¼ cups flour â‰ˆ 150g, 1 cup cornmeal â‰ˆ 140g, 1 tbsp baking powder â‰ˆ 12g, 1Â½ tsp salt â‰ˆ 7g, 4 eggs â‰ˆ 200g, Â¾-1 cup jalapeÃ±os â‰ˆ 120g, 1 cup corn â‰ˆ 150g, Â½ cup cream â‰ˆ 120g, 1 cup cheese â‰ˆ 113g, Â¾ cup butter â‰ˆ 170g, â…“-â…” cup sugar â‰ˆ 100g
Reduce salt if using salted butter
Can reheat covered with foil at 350Â°F for 8-10 minutes
Cast iron skillet creates best crust

recipes.title: Jambalaya

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-size pieces
andouille or smoked sausage, cut into 1/2-inch thick rounds

steps: Make Creole seasoning: Place garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, white pepper, and cayenne in a small bowl and stir to combine.
Place diced onion, celery, bell pepper, and minced garlic in a medium bowl.
Cut chicken thighs into bite-size pieces. Season the chicken with 4.5g of the kosher salt and 1 tablespoon of the Creole seasoning. Cut sausage into 1/2-inch thick rounds.
Heat 14g of the oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until shimmering. You'll know the oil is hot enough when a sprinkle of water pops and crackles.
Add the sausage in a single layer and cook until browned on the cut sides, 3 to 4 minutes per side. Using tongs, transfer the sausage to a plate.
Add the remaining 14g oil to the drippings in the pot. Add the chicken and cook until browned, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Using tongs, transfer the chicken to the plate with the sausage.

Add the vegetable mixture, remaining Creole seasoning, and remaining 4.5g kosher salt. Cook, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom of the pot and stirring occasionally, until the onions are translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add rice and cook, stirring frequently, until the rice is opaque and toasty smelling, about 3 minutes.
Pour in water or chicken broth and bring to a boil. Return the chicken and sausage and any accumulated juices to the pot and give everything one good stir to mix together.
Cover, reduce the heat to low, and simmer undisturbed until the rice is cooked through, 20 to 30 minutes.

Give the mixture a gentle stir on top. Cover again, remove from the heat, and let sit for 10 minutes. If the rice is still too wet, take the lid off so the extra liquid evaporates. If the rice is a little dry, keep the lid on a little longer to give the rice more time to absorb the liquid.
Once rice is at desired consistency, fluff and serve. Garnish with scallions and a few dashes of vinegar-based hot sauce.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tbsp garlic powder â‰ˆ 10g, 1 tbsp onion powder â‰ˆ 8g, 1 tbsp paprika â‰ˆ 7g, 1 tsp white pepper â‰ˆ 2g, 1/2 tsp cayenne â‰ˆ 1g, 1 small onion â‰ˆ 150g, 3 celery stalks â‰ˆ 150g, 1 bell pepper â‰ˆ 150g, 3 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 9g, 12 oz chicken â‰ˆ 340g, 12 oz sausage â‰ˆ 340g, 1Â½ tsp salt â‰ˆ 9g, 2 tbsp oil â‰ˆ 28g, 2 cups rice â‰ˆ 400g, 2Â½ cups broth â‰ˆ 600g, 2 scallions â‰ˆ 20g
The Creole seasoning can be made up to 1 month ahead and stored in an airtight container
Leftovers can be refrigerated in an airtight container up to 4 days

recipes.title: Keto Bacon Meatloaf
steps: Preheat oven to 400Â°F (205Â°C). Grease a medium baking dish with cooking spray.
Heat olive oil in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add onion and celery. Cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
Stir in garlic, oregano, and chili powder. Cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Let vegetable mixture cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine ground beef, cooled vegetable mixture, cheddar cheese, almond flour, Parmesan, eggs, and soy sauce.
Season generously with salt and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Shape mixture into a large loaf in the prepared baking dish.
Lay bacon strips on top of the meatloaf.
Bake until bacon is crispy and beef is cooked through, about 1 hour. If bacon is cooking too quickly, cover dish with foil.
Let rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing and serving.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 14g, 1 medium onion â‰ˆ 150g, 1 celery stalk â‰ˆ 40g, 3 garlic cloves â‰ˆ 15g, 1 tsp oregano â‰ˆ 3g, 1 tsp chili powder â‰ˆ 3g, 2 lb ground beef â‰ˆ 907g, 1 cup cheddar â‰ˆ 113g, Â½ cup almond flour â‰ˆ 60g, Â¼ cup Parmesan â‰ˆ 28g, 2 eggs â‰ˆ 100g, 1 tbsp soy sauce â‰ˆ 15g, 6 bacon strips â‰ˆ 180g
Keto-friendly with almond flour instead of breadcrumbs
Cover with foil if bacon browns before meatloaf is cooked through
Per serving: 450 calories, 46g protein, 6g carbs, 2g fiber, 27g fat

recipes.title: Khachapuri (Georgian Cheese Bread)
steps: In bowl of stand mixer, combine salt, yeast, sugar, and flour.
Heat water and milk to about 115Â°F (46Â°C). Pour into bowl of dry ingredients.
Knead with hook attachment until close to smooth and elastic.
Add oil and knead for another minute.
Drizzle olive oil into a deep bowl. Place dough inside, cover with plastic wrap, and set in warm place until doubled in size (about 1 hour).
Press into dough a few times. Cover with plastic wrap again and let sit in warm place for another 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine farmers cheese, feta, and mozzarella in a bowl.
Remove dough and place on floured surface. Cut into 4 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a 9-inch circle. Roll 2 opposite sides toward the center to create boat shape. Pinch corners together.
Transfer khachapuri onto baking sheet lined with greased parchment paper.
Stuff each khachapuri with cheese mixture.
Beat 1 egg with 1 tsp water. Brush dough with egg wash.
Bake in preheated 450Â°F (230Â°C) oven for about 15 minutes until crust is golden brown.
Make a 3-inch well in center of each khachapuri with back of spoon. Drop 1 egg into each well.
Stick small pieces of butter into the cheese.
Return to oven and bake for another 5-6 minutes. Egg white should be white but still runny.
When serving, mix cheese and egg with a fork. Serve immediately.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tsp salt â‰ˆ 5g, 1 tsp yeast â‰ˆ 7g, 1 tbsp sugar â‰ˆ 12g, 3Â½ cups flour â‰ˆ 420g, 1 cup water â‰ˆ 240g, Â½ cup milk â‰ˆ 120g, 1 tbsp oil â‰ˆ 14g, 1Â½ cups farmers cheese â‰ˆ 180g, 1Â½ cups mozzarella â‰ˆ 170g, 1Â½ cups feta â‰ˆ 180g, 4 eggs â‰ˆ 200g, 1 egg â‰ˆ 50g
Traditional Georgian dish
Egg will continue to cook in hot cheese after removing from oven
Serve immediately while hot and cheese is melted

recipes.title: Homemade Kimchi

ingredients.name: onion (1 large per 2 heads of cabbage)

steps: Brine the cabbage: Quarter napa cabbage lengthwise. Dissolve a generous amount of coarse sea salt in water. Submerge cabbage in salt water and place a weight on top to keep it submerged.
Let brine for 6 hours, turning the cabbage a couple times during brining. The cabbage should be very salty when you taste it - don't worry, it will be rinsed.
After 6 hours, rinse the cabbage thoroughly under cold water to remove salt. The cabbage should bend without breaking when properly brined. Let drain completely.
Make the paste base: Blend Korean pear, onion (1 large onion per 2 heads of cabbage), garlic, and ginger until smooth.
Make glutinous rice flour mixture: Mix glutinous rice flour with water and beef stock powder. Cook until thickened, then cool completely.
Mix the paste: Combine the blended pear mixture, cooled rice flour mixture, gochugaru (use high quality Korean gochugaru like Nongshim brand), fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and a tiny bit of MSG.
Add chopped spring onions to the paste.
If making radish kimchi, divide the paste and add radish sticks to one portion.
Wearing gloves, coat each cabbage leaf thoroughly with the paste, working from the outer leaves inward.
Pack the kimchi tightly into clean jars or containers. Press down to remove air pockets.
Let ferment at room temperature for 1-3 days depending on desired sourness, then refrigerate.
Kimchi will continue to ferment slowly in the refrigerator and develop flavor over time.
notes: Recipe adapted from Maangchi and Baek Jongwon's methods
The key to good kimchi is generous salting - the cabbage should taste too salty before rinsing
Use high-quality Korean gochugaru (not Chinese substitutes) for best flavor and color - Nongshim brand recommended
Korean pear adds natural sweetness and is healthier than adding extra sugar
Measurements are intentionally flexible - adjust to taste as you go
1 large onion â‰ˆ 200g

recipes.title: Sous Vide Limoncello

ingredients.name: high proof grain alcohol (96% vol / 192 US proof)

steps: Set sous vide cooker to 134.6Â°F (57.0Â°C).
Using a tomato peeler or lemon zester, remove only the yellow peel from 10 lemons, avoiding the bitter white pith.
Add lemon peel to high-proof grain alcohol. Divide 5 lemon peels into each 500ml jar of alcohol for optimal flavor extraction.
Seal jars and immerse in the water bath for 3 hours while the lemon zest macerates.
While macerating, prepare simple syrup by dissolving 1400g sugar in 2000g water. Boil for 2 minutes, then cool completely.
After maceration, shake jars and cool to room temperature.
Strain through coffee filters or cheesecloth, discarding peel.
Divide the strained alcohol (approximately 333ml per bottle) into three clean 1-liter bottles.
Fill each bottle to 1 liter capacity with simple syrup (about 666ml per bottle), resulting in 32% alcohol by volume.
Keep in a cool, dark, dry place for several weeks before serving to allow flavors to develop.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 liter alcohol â‰ˆ 1000g, 2 liters water â‰ˆ 2000g
Use organic lemons to avoid pesticides in the peel
Do not use vodka - high-proof grain alcohol is essential
Final product yields approximately 3 liters at 32% ABV
Improves with age - wait at least a few weeks before drinking

recipes.title: Mango Chutney
steps: Heat oil over medium-high in a medium stock pot.
SautÃ© ginger, garlic, and red chilies for 1 minute.
Add nigella seeds, coriander, cumin, turmeric, cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon. SautÃ© for another minute.
Add diced mangoes, sugar, salt, and vinegar. Stir to combine.
Bring to a rapid boil, then reduce heat to medium-low.
Simmer steadily for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and allow to cool.
If you prefer smaller chunks, use a potato masher to mash the mixture to desired consistency.
Store in sealed jars in refrigerator for up to 2 months, or freeze for several months.
For best results, let chutney sit for a few days before using to allow flavors to develop and mellow.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tbsp oil â‰ˆ 14g, 2 tsp ginger â‰ˆ 8g, 2 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 10g, 2 tsp nigella seeds â‰ˆ 6g, 1 tsp coriander â‰ˆ 3g, Â½ tsp cumin â‰ˆ 2g, spices â‰ˆ 2g each, 5 large mangoes â‰ˆ 1500g total, 2 cups sugar â‰ˆ 400g, 1 cup vinegar â‰ˆ 240g
Nigella seeds are optional but highly recommended for incredible flavor
Remove chili seeds and membrane for less heat
Can substitute frozen mango (1500g total)
Makes roughly 2Â½ pints of chutney
Canning instructions: Pour hot mixture into sterilized jars and process in water bath for 10 minutes. Store in dark, cool place for at least 1 year

recipes.title: Mango Kombucha

ingredients.name: black tea bags or loose leaf tea
green tea bags or loose leaf tea

steps: Bring half of the water (1660g / 7 cups) to a boil in a Dutch oven or large pot.
Remove from heat and stir in sugar to dissolve.
Drop in the tea bags and steep until the water is completely cooled (a few hours).
Stir in the remaining water (1660g / 7 cups).
Remove and discard tea bags. If using loose leaf tea, strain out the tea leaves.
Stir in the pre-made kombucha.
Pour the mixture into a clean 1-gallon or larger glass jar.
With clean hands, gently place the SCOBY on top.
Cover the mouth of the jar with a clean cotton dish towel, cheesecloth, or paper towels secured with a rubber band.
Store at room temperature, out of direct sunlight, where jar won't get jostled.
Ferment for 7-10 days. A new cream-colored SCOBY layer should form on the surface within a few days. Brown stringy bits, sediment, and bubbles are all normal.
After 7 days, begin tasting daily. When it reaches a pleasant balance of sweetness and tartness, it's ready to bottle.
With clean hands, gently lift the SCOBY out and set on a clean plate. If making another batch right away, measure out starter tea and set aside.
Divide the diced mango between bottles.
Pour fermented kombucha into bottles using a small funnel, leaving about 1 inch of head room.
Seal the bottles and store at room temperature out of direct sunlight.
Allow 1-3 days for carbonation. Plastic bottles will be rock-solid when carbonated; for glass bottles, open one intermittently to check.
Once carbonated, refrigerate for at least 4 hours to chill.
Strain the mango when serving if desired. Kombucha will keep refrigerated for several weeks.
notes: Volume conversions: 3Â½ quarts water = 3320g total (divided into two 1660g portions), 1 cup sugar â‰ˆ 200g, 4 tea bags â‰ˆ 1 tbsp loose leaf â‰ˆ 8g, 2 cups kombucha â‰ˆ 480g, 1 cup mango â‰ˆ 150g
Makes about 1 gallon
Total time is approximately 10 days (7-10 days primary fermentation + 1-3 days carbonation)
SCOBY may float at top, bottom, or sideways during fermentation - all positions are normal
Thawed frozen mango works fine

recipes.title: Masala Chaas (Spiced Buttermilk)

ingredients.name: fresh cilantro or mint for garnish

steps: Add all ingredients to a blender: yogurt, water, green chili, ginger, cilantro, cumin, black salt, salt, and chat masala.
Blend until combined and smooth.
Pour into serving glasses.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or mint.
Serve immediately, chilled.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 cup yogurt â‰ˆ 245g, 2 cups water â‰ˆ 480g, 1 green chili â‰ˆ 5g, Â½ inch ginger â‰ˆ 5g, 1 tbsp cilantro â‰ˆ 4g, Â½ tsp cumin â‰ˆ 2g, Â½ tsp black salt â‰ˆ 3g, Â¼ tsp salt â‰ˆ 2g, Â¼ tsp chat masala â‰ˆ 2g
Perfect for cooling off during summers
Can be prepared in advance and refrigerated
Tastes best when chilled

recipes.title: Mohinga (Burmese Fish Noodle Soup)
steps: Toast rice at 350Â°F (175Â°C) for 20 minutes until golden. Grind to powder.
Boil water with lemongrass, ginger piece, bay leaves, black pepper, white pepper, and salt for 15 minutes.
Add catfish and simmer 15 minutes. Turn fish and simmer 5 more minutes until flesh separates from bone.
Remove fish, cool, discard skin, separate meat from bones. Return skeleton to pot.
Simmer skeleton 15 minutes. Strain broth (should yield about 10 cups).
Whisk rice powder with a bit of broth, then stir into pot. Simmer 5 minutes until slightly thickened.
Heat oil in pan. Stir-fry lemongrass stalk (minced), minced garlic, and minced ginger for 1 minute.
Add fish meat with paprika and turmeric. Mash into paste and cook 1 minute.
Pour fish mixture into broth. Add onions and fish sauce. Simmer 5 minutes. Adjust seasoning.
Cook noodles 5-6 minutes. Drain and rinse.
Divide noodles among bowls. Ladle soup over top.
Serve with halved hard-boiled eggs, crackers, cilantro, and lime.
notes: Volume conversions: Â½ cup rice â‰ˆ 95g, 3 quarts water â‰ˆ 2840g, 3 stalks lemongrass â‰ˆ 45g, 2 oz ginger â‰ˆ 57g, 1Â½ tsp black pepper â‰ˆ 4g, Â½ tsp white pepper â‰ˆ 2g, 2 tsp salt â‰ˆ 10g, â…“ cup oil â‰ˆ 79g, Â¼ cup garlic â‰ˆ 40g, 3 tbsp ginger â‰ˆ 45g
The trick is achieving the right balance of ingredients - fresh fish and lemongrass counteract fishy aroma
Traditional Burmese breakfast dish

recipes.title: Octopus Taco with Pineapple and Salsa de Semilla

ingredients.name: Masa Mole Mexican Blend or taco seasoning

steps: Blanch octopus in boiling water by dipping the entire octopus in the water for 3 seconds and then pulling it out. Repeat this process for a total of 3 dips.
Sous vide octopus tentacles with a splash of oil and a pinch of salt at 175Â°F for 5 hours. Alternatively you can continue to boil octopus until tender, about an hour or so.
When the octopus is cooked, you can either dip them into an ice bath to stop cooking and set the skin, or let it cool completely in the cooking liquid if time permits.
While the octopus cools, make your salsas and heat your grill.
For Salsa de Semilla: Heat olive oil in a sauce pan over medium heat and add garlic to toast.
When garlic is light brown, add sunflower seeds, pine nuts, sesame seeds, and cumin seeds. Continue to toast until fragrant, 30 seconds or so. Remove from the heat.
For Pineapple Salsa: Toss diced pineapple with chopped cilantro and lime juice. Season to taste with salt.
Remove the tentacles from the body of the octopus if you boiled (they should already be removed if you used sous vide).
Toss tentacles with just enough olive oil to coat and season them generously with Masa Mole and salt.
Gently and carefully place them on the well oiled and clean grill so they do not stick. They are already cooked so we just want a quick hot sear to char them a bit.
Assemble your taco: Tortilla, then beans, then octopus, and finish with both of your salsas.
notes: Volume conversions: 2-3 lbs octopus â‰ˆ 1200g, 3 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 42g, 2 tbsp Masa Mole â‰ˆ 30g, 1/4 cup sunflower seeds â‰ˆ 35g, 1/4 cup pine nuts â‰ˆ 35g, 1/4 cup sesame seeds â‰ˆ 35g, 1 tsp cumin â‰ˆ 2g, 2 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 6g, 1/2 pineapple â‰ˆ 300g, 1 bunch cilantro â‰ˆ 25g, 1 lime juice â‰ˆ 30g
The triple dip blanch technique is a classic Galician technique used throughout Spain - it slowly tempers the tentacles and prevents them from tensing up and getting tough
Sous vide is a modern cooking technique where you vacuum seal your ingredients and dip them into water heated to an exact temperature
Inspired by the octopus tacos of Sayulita, Mexico - reminiscent of tacos al pastor but with octopus instead of pork

recipes.title: Panzanella Salad

ingredients.name: mixed ripe tomatoes, cut into bite-size pieces
ciabatta or rustic sourdough bread, cut into 1Â½-inch cubes
white wine vinegar or red wine vinegar

steps: Place chopped tomatoes in a colander set over a bowl and season with 8g kosher salt. Toss to coat. Set aside at room temperature to drain, tossing occasionally, for a minimum of 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, preheat oven to 350Â°F (180Â°C) and adjust rack to center position. In a large bowl, toss bread cubes with 30g olive oil. Transfer to a rimmed baking sheet.
Bake about 15 minutes, until crisp and firm but not browned. Remove from oven and let cool.
Remove colander with tomatoes from bowl with tomato juice. Place colander with tomatoes in sink.
Add shallot, garlic, mustard, and vinegar to bowl with tomato juice. Whisking constantly, drizzle in remaining 120g olive oil. Season dressing to taste with salt and pepper.
Combine toasted bread, tomatoes, and dressing in a large bowl. Add basil leaves. Toss everything to coat and season with salt and pepper.
Let rest 30 minutes before serving, tossing occasionally until the dressing is absorbed by the bread.
notes: Use a hearty, open-structured bread, like ciabatta or sourdough

Volume conversions: 2Â½ lbs tomatoes â‰ˆ 1100g, 2 tsp salt â‰ˆ 8g, Â¾ lb bread â‰ˆ 340g, 10 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 150g, 1 small shallot â‰ˆ 30g, 2 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 6g, Â½ tsp mustard â‰ˆ 3g, 2 tbsp vinegar â‰ˆ 30g, Â½ cup basil â‰ˆ 20g

recipes.title: Pomegranate Wine

ingredients.name: simple syrup or pomegranate juice (optional, for sweetening)

steps: Day 1: Sanitize all equipment, work area, and hands.
Wash and peel 6-8 large pomegranates, removing peel and membranes. Keep only the arils (seeds).
Mash the pomegranate arils using a potato masher or ricer over a fermenting bucket to catch all juice.
Blend white grapes in a food processor to extract juice. Add to bucket.
Add sugar to a stock pot with 1 gallon (3785g) water. Whisk and simmer over low heat until sugar dissolves and syrup is hot but not boiling.
Pour hot sugar water over mashed fruit.
Once cool, add pomegranate molasses, acid blend, tannin, and pectic enzyme.
Crush 1 campden tablet between two spoons and stir into the must. Take an SG reading with hydrometer (optional).
Cover bucket with breathable fabric secured with rubber band. Set aside for 24 hours.
Day 2: Using a sterilized spoon, stir yeast nutrient and half of the yeast packet into the must. Cover and set aside.
Day 3+: Stir daily and take SG readings.
When SG reaches 1.030 (day 4-7), remove mesh bag from bucket, squeeze juice back in, discard seeds.
Rack (transfer) juice into a 1-gallon carboy, leaving space for expansion.
Fill airlock halfway with sanitizer solution or water. Fit airlock into bung or screw cap.
Store carboy away from heat and direct sunlight between 70-85Â°F.
After 3 weeks, when SG is around 1.000, transfer wine into clean carboy, leaving sediment behind.
Allow wine to clear 1-3 months, re-racking into clean carboy each time.
Once clear, taste wine. If sweetening is desired, add Â¼-â…“ cup simple syrup or pomegranate juice and Â½ tsp wine stabilizer.
Take final SG reading and calculate ABV using formula: (Original SG - Final SG) Ã— 131.25 = ABV%.
Add 1 crushed campden tablet to wine and stir until dissolved.
Bottle the wine and label with type, date, and alcohol percentage.
Age for at least 6 months before enjoying.
notes: Volume conversions: 6-8 pomegranates yield about 5 cups arils, 1 gallon water â‰ˆ 3785g, 2 cups grapes â‰ˆ 300g, 4 cups sugar â‰ˆ 907g, 2 tbsp molasses â‰ˆ 30g, 2 tsp acid blend â‰ˆ 6g, 1 tsp pectic enzyme â‰ˆ 3g, Â¼ tsp tannin â‰ˆ 2g, Â½ packet yeast â‰ˆ 3g, 1Â½ tsp nutrient â‰ˆ 5g
Total time is approximately 90 days minimum (3 months fermenting/clearing + 6 months aging)
Taking SG readings on day 1 and before bottling allows ABV calculation (optional but recommended)
Pour extra must in smaller bottle with airlock to top off larger jar during re-racking
Add sediment to compost bin or dilute with water for garden use
Serve chilled rather than at room temperature
Makes approximately 1 gallon (25 wine glasses)
Flip-top bottles are a cheaper alternative to wine bottles, corks, and corker

recipes.title: Pot de CrÃ¨me
steps: Heat sous vide water bath to 176Â°F (80Â°C).
Combine egg yolks, salt, and sugar in a bowl and whisk until smooth.
Add cream to the base and whisk until smooth.
Strain the mixture, then allow to rest for 20 to 30 minutes so that bubbles have time to rise to the top and dissipate. Skim away any remaining bubbles.
Cast 150g of mixture into each wide-mouth canning jar, pouring in a slow, low, steady stream. Pouring too quickly or from too high will cause bubbles.
Place lids on jars and twist until 'fingertip tight' - just barely closed. This allows air to escape during cooking and prevents cracking.
Using canning tongs, carefully transfer jars to sous vide bath, ensuring they are fully submerged. Cook for 1 hour.
Remove jars from bath. Rest at room temperature for 15 minutes before transferring to an ice bath (or just refrigerate). Rapid temperature change can crack the jars.
Once cold, jars will keep sealed in the refrigerator for up to a week.
Serve as is or topped with maple syrup and flake salt.
notes: Similar to crÃ¨me brÃ»lÃ©e but richer and more unctuous - has almost twice the egg yolks.
Cooking sous vide means you can make as many servings as you like with consistent results.
Maple syrup and flake salt is an alarmingly delicious topping combination.
Black lava salt makes a gorgeous color contrast if you have it.
Skip the ice bath if you prefer - just chill in the fridge instead.
To close jars fingertip tight: twist until you feel resistance, then twist back once, then forward once more.

recipes.title: Qamar al-Deen (Apricot Drink)
steps: Cut the apricot leather into small pieces (approximately 2-inch squares).
Place in a deep bowl and cover with water.
Refrigerate overnight (at least 8 hours).
Blend the apricot with water and sugar until no lumps remain.
Strain if desired for smoother texture.
Pour into a pitcher and refrigerate until serving.
Serve cold.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 package (400g) apricot leather, 3 cups water â‰ˆ 710g, â…“ cup sugar â‰ˆ 67g
Famous Ramadan beverage from the Middle East
Water and sugar amounts can be adjusted to taste
Homemade apricot leather can be substituted

recipes.title: Ramen Bowl - Master Recipe

ingredients.name: BROTH - Choose one: Tonkotsu (pork bone, 18hr boil), Chicken Chintan (clear, 6hr simmer), Chicken Paitan (creamy, 8hr boil), or Vegetable broth
TARE (seasoning) - Choose one: Shoyu (soy sauce based, 30-40ml), Shio (salt based, 30-40ml), or Miso (paste, 40-50g)
NOODLES - Fresh ramen noodles (wheat + kansui alkaline salts), thickness varies by style
AROMA OIL - Choose one: Chicken fat, pork lard, scallion oil, or mayu (black garlic oil)
TOPPINGS - Soft boiled egg (6-7 min), marinated in soy/mirin brine, halved
TOPPINGS - Optional: menma (bamboo shoots), nori (seaweed), corn, bean sprouts, spinach, wood ear mushroom

steps: PREPARE COMPONENTS IN ADVANCE: Make your chosen broth (6-18 hours depending on type), tare (30-90 min), aroma oil (30-60 min), chashu (4-12 hours), and marinated eggs (overnight). Fresh noodles need 24-48 hours rest after making. Store components separately in fridge until assembly.
HEAT BROTH: In a pot, heat your chosen broth to 95-100Â°C (203-212Â°F) until steaming hot. You need 350-400ml per bowl.
PREHEAT BOWL: Fill your serving bowl with boiling water to preheat it. This is critical - cold bowls cool the soup too quickly. Let sit while you prepare noodles.

BOIL NOODLES: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil (no salt needed). Add 130-150g fresh noodles and cook for 1-3 minutes depending on thickness. Stir gently to separate. Thin noodles (Hakata): 1-2 min. Medium noodles (Tokyo): 2-3 min. Thick noodles (Jiro, Tsukemen): 3-4 min.

ASSEMBLE BOWL BASE: Dump water from serving bowl. Add tare to empty bowl: 30-40ml for shoyu/shio, or 40-50g for miso. Add aroma oil (about 1 tablespoon/15ml). Pour hot broth over tare and oil, stir gently to combine.
DRAIN AND ADD NOODLES: Drain noodles thoroughly and shake off excess water. Add to bowl and arrange neatly in the center, using chopsticks to separate and fold if desired.
TOP AND SERVE: Arrange toppings artfully: chashu slices, halved soft-boiled egg, sliced green onions, and any optional toppings. Serve immediately while hot.
notes: POPULAR COMBINATIONS:
â€¢ Shoyu Ramen: Chicken chintan + shoyu tare + scallion-niboshi oil + medium noodles + standard toppings
â€¢ Miso Ramen: Chicken-pork chintan + miso tare + ginger-onion lard + thick wavy noodles + corn, butter
â€¢ Tonkotsu: Pork bone paitan + shio or light shoyu tare + scallion oil + thin straight noodles + pickled ginger, sesame
â€¢ Tsukemen (dipping): Ultra-thick concentrated soup served separate from noodles for dipping
BROTH OPTIONS: Chintan (clear, delicate) vs Paitan (cloudy, rich from emulsified collagen). Tonkotsu is pork bone paitan boiled 18+ hours.
TARE is the soul of ramen - it provides ALL the salt. Without it, broth is bland. Typical ratio: 10% tare to broth volume.

NOODLES must contain wheat flour + kansui (alkaline salts). This gives them yellow color and springy texture. Store-bought Sun Noodle brand works great. Fresh noodles improve after 1-2 days rest in fridge.
TIMING: All components except noodles and assembly can be made days in advance. Final assembly takes only 5 minutes. Broths freeze well for 6 months.
Source: The Ramen_Lord Book of Ramen by Mike Satinover (Ramen_Lord) - comprehensive guide to ramen components and techniques

recipes.title: Ramen Bowl - Build Your Own

options.name: Tonkotsu (Creamy Pork Bone)

ingredients.name: Pork femurs, split to expose marrow

steps: Soak bones in cold water for 6 hours or overnight to remove blood (optional but improves color).
Add bones to fresh water, bring to boil, then simmer 15-20 minutes while skimming scum continuously. This blanch is critical for white tonkotsu.
Strain and discard blanching liquid. Scrub bones clean under running water.
Add clean bones to pot with 6L fresh water. Boil at medium heat for 18 hours, stirring frequently and adding water to maintain coverage.
If using aromatics, add in last hour of cooking.
Strain soup. For final creamy emulsion, blend soup in blender on high for 30 seconds (or use immersion blender for 5 minutes).
Yields ~3L. Use 350ml per bowl. Freezes well for 6 months.

options.name: Chicken Chintan (Clear)

ingredients.name: Chicken backs (or 2 small whole chickens, broken down)

steps: Add chicken to stockpot, cover with water.
Bring to boil briefly and skim any scum for 5-10 minutes.
Reduce heat to below simmer (88Â°C/190Â°F) and hold for 6 hours.
If using aromatics, add for final hour.
Strain and reserve. Yields ~3L clear, delicate broth. Use 350ml per bowl.

options.name: Chicken Paitan (Creamy)
steps: Add chicken parts and water to pot.
Bring to boil, then simmer and skim scum for 15-20 minutes.
Boil at medium heat for 6 hours, stirring frequently and adding water to maintain coverage.
Add aromatics and boil thoroughly for final hour.
Strain soup. Blend in blender on high for 30 seconds to finalize creamy emulsion (or immersion blend for 5 minutes).
Yields ~4L rich, creamy chicken broth. Use 350ml per bowl.

options.name: Vegetable Chintan (Vegan)
steps: Add water, onion, carrot, garlic, ginger, and shiitake to pot.
Bring to boil, then reduce to simmer.
Cook at simmer (95Â°C/203Â°F) for 1 hour.
Add green onion and kombu, turn off heat, steep 15 minutes.
Strain and reserve. Yields ~2.5L light, aromatic vegan broth. Pairs best with miso tare. Use 350ml per bowl.

options.name: Shoyu (Soy Sauce)
steps: Combine soy sauce, mirin, kombu, niboshi, and sake in sealed container.
Refrigerate 6 hours to 2 days to cold-steep.
Heat to 71Â°C/160Â°F and hold 10 minutes.
Remove and discard kombu.
Bring to 82Â°C/180Â°F (just below boil) and hold 15 minutes.
Add katsuobushi, hold at 82Â°C/180Â°F for 5 more minutes.
Add sugar, whisk to dissolve.
Strain and refrigerate up to 6 months. Use 30ml tare per 350ml broth.

options.name: Shio (Salt-Based)
steps: Combine water, kombu, and mirin in sealed container.
Refrigerate 6 hours to 2 days.
Heat to 71Â°C/160Â°F and steep 10 minutes.
Remove and discard kombu.
Add sake, bring to boil, cook 10 minutes until alcohol smell subsides.
Add salt and MSG, whisk to dissolve.
Cool before use. Stores indefinitely refrigerated. Use 30ml tare per 350ml broth.

options.name: Miso (Fermented Soybean Paste)

ingredients.name: Onion, pureed or finely grated (about 1/2 onion)

steps: Take 70g of pureed onion and add to small pan.
Cook over low heat, stirring frequently, until golden brown (20-30 minutes).
Remove from heat and place in mixing bowl to cool.
When cooled, add remaining raw onion (70g), garlic, ginger, all misos, mirin, soy sauce, sesame oil, tahini, black pepper, and tobanjan.
Whisk thoroughly to combine into smooth paste.
Refrigerate in covered container for at least 24 hours to develop flavor and mellow raw vegetables.
Keeps up to 12 months refrigerated. Use 60-70g tare per 350ml broth.

options.name: Chicken Fat (Neutral)
steps: MICROWAVE METHOD: Place chicken skin in microwave-safe bowl. Cook uncovered on high for 8 minutes. Strain fat from crispy skins.
STOVETOP METHOD: Add skin and 15-30g water to pan. Heat over medium, stirring occasionally for 45 minutes until fat renders and skin crisps. Strain.
Store refrigerated up to 6 months. Use ~15ml (1 tbsp) per bowl.

options.name: Pork Lard (Rich)
steps: If using store-bought lard, melt and use as-is. If rendering from backfat:
Preheat oven to 121Â°C/250Â°F.
Add backfat and 15-30g water to oven-safe pan.
Cook 2-3 hours until golden brown and fully rendered.
Strain and reserve. Store refrigerated up to 6 months. Use ~15ml (1 tbsp) per bowl.

options.name: Scallion Oil (Aromatic)
steps: Add oil and scallion whites to small saucepan.
Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until scallions turn light golden and fragrant (10-15 minutes).
Remove from heat and let cool in pot.
Strain and transfer to container. Store refrigerated up to 6 months. Use ~15ml (1 tbsp) per bowl.

options.name: Mayu (Black Garlic Oil)

ingredients.name: Oil (mix of sesame, vegetable, and lard)

steps: Mince garlic finely in food processor (or by hand to 1/8-inch pieces).
Spread mince on paper towels and squeeze out as much moisture as possible. Replace towels until dry.
Heat oil in pan. When warm, add garlic.
Cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until very dark brown (almost black) and oil begins to smoke (15-20 minutes).
Strain solids, reserving oil. Lay solids on paper towels to remove excess oil until crispy and crumbly.
Add dried solids to mortar and pestle. Mash for ~5 minutes until oil escapes and forms smooth black paste.
Mix reserved oil back in to thin to desired consistency.
Store refrigerated up to 6 months. Use sparingly (~5-10ml per bowl) for visual contrast and bitter complexity.

options.name: Chili Oil (Spicy)
steps: Add fat, garlic, ginger, and onion to small saucepan.
Heat over medium-low, stirring occasionally, until ingredients turn light golden and fragrant (15 minutes).
Add togarashi, Chinese chilis, and Sichuan peppercorns.
Remove from heat and steep 30 minutes.
Strain and transfer to container. Store refrigerated up to 6 months. Use ~15ml (1 tbsp) per bowl for heat.

options.name: Chashu (Braised Pork Belly)
steps: Preheat oven to 110Â°C/225Â°F.
Heat oven-safe pot over medium. Season pork with salt and pepper.
Sear pork on all sides until golden brown (4-5 min per side). Sop up rendered fat with paper towel.
Combine mirin, soy sauce, water, brown sugar, and garlic in bowl.
Deglaze pot with sake, then add soy mixture.
Bring to boil, cover, and braise in oven 3-4 hours, turning hourly, until tender (internal temp ~93Â°C/200Â°F).
Cool in braising liquid, refrigerate until cold for easy slicing.
Slice and optionally sear before serving. Use 2-3 slices per bowl.

options.name: Soft Boiled Eggs (Marinated)
steps: Bring large pot of water to rolling boil. Prepare ice bath.
Optional: poke small hole in wider end of egg with thumbtack (prevents cracking, aids peeling).
Lower eggs into boiling water (4-6 at a time to maintain temp).
Boil 6-7 minutes, stirring gently first minute. 6 min = liquid yolk, 7 min = slightly custardy yolk.
Immediately transfer to ice bath for 15 minutes until fully chilled.
Peel eggs carefully.
QUICK BRINE: Combine equal parts soy sauce, mirin, and water. Add peeled eggs, jostle every hour, soak 4-6 hours. Remove and refrigerate.
Halve eggs before serving. Use 1/2 egg per bowl.

options.name: Green Onions (Sliced)
steps: Slice green onions into thin rings, oblong diamonds (45Â° angle), or fine threads.
Use both white and green parts for color contrast.
Serve raw as garnish. Use ~10g per bowl.

options.name: Menma (Bamboo Shoots)

ingredients.name: Lacto-fermented bamboo shoots (or canned as substitute)

steps: If using fermented bamboo: soak in cold water 12-24 hours in fridge. Drain thoroughly.
Add bamboo, mirin, sugar, and soy sauce to sautÃ© pan.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until liquid slightly absorbed (10-15 minutes).
Add sesame oil to coat.
Transfer to container and refrigerate 24 hours before using. Keeps for months. Use 20-30g per bowl.

options.name: Nori (Seaweed Sheet)
steps: Cut nori sheet into rectangles or squares.
Place on edge of bowl just before serving so it stays crispy.
Use 1 sheet per bowl.

options.name: Corn (Sweet)
steps: If using frozen corn, thaw and drain.
If using canned corn, drain well.
Optional: quickly sautÃ© in butter for extra flavor.
Sprinkle on top of finished bowl. Use ~30-50g per bowl.

options.name: Salt Cured Egg Yolk (Grated)
steps: Pour Â½ inch of salt into a glass or stoneware baking dish.
Use a spoon to create small indentations for each yolk, keeping them Â¼ inch apart.
Carefully separate eggs and place yolks in divots.
Cover yolks completely with salt (should be invisible under salt).
Refrigerate for 7 days.
Remove yolks from salt and dust off excess with a damp towel.
Wrap yolks in cheesecloth, separated by butcher's twine to maintain airflow.
Hang in a cool location (below 50Â°F, preferably in refrigerator) for 7-10 days.
Yolks should be firm but still grate-able (not rock hard).
Store tightly covered in refrigerator. Grate over finished ramen bowl.
PREHEAT BOWL: Fill serving bowl with boiling water. Let sit while preparing - this is critical to prevent cooling the soup.
HEAT BROTH: Bring your selected broth to 95-100Â°C (203-212Â°F) in a pot.

BOIL NOODLES: Bring large pot of unsalted water to rolling boil. Add 140g fresh noodles. Cook 1-3 minutes depending on thickness (thin: 1-2 min, medium: 2-3 min, thick: 3-4 min). Stir gently to separate.
ASSEMBLE BOWL BASE: Dump preheating water from bowl. Add your selected tare amount to empty bowl (shoyu/shio: 30ml, miso: 60-70g).
Add your selected aroma oil (~15ml or 1 tablespoon).
Pour hot broth (350ml) over tare and oil. Stir gently to combine and dissolve tare completely.
DRAIN AND ADD NOODLES: Drain noodles thoroughly, shake off excess water. Add to bowl in center, using chopsticks to arrange neatly if desired.
TOP AND SERVE: Arrange your selected toppings artfully: chashu slices, halved egg, green onions, and any additional toppings.
SERVE IMMEDIATELY while piping hot. Enjoy your custom ramen creation!

notes: MODULAR DESIGN: This recipe lets you build your perfect bowl by mixing and matching components. Each component can be made 1-7 days in advance.
POPULAR COMBINATIONS:
â€¢ Classic Shoyu: Chicken Chintan + Shoyu Tare + Scallion Oil + Chashu + Egg + Green Onions
â€¢ Rich Tonkotsu: Tonkotsu + Shio Tare + Pork Lard + Chashu + Egg + Green Onions + Mayu drizzle
â€¢ Sapporo Miso: Chicken Paitan + Miso Tare + Pork Lard + Corn + Chashu + Green Onions
â€¢ Vegan: Vegetable Chintan + Miso Tare + Scallion Oil + Corn + Green Onions + Nori
MAKE-AHEAD SCHEDULE:
â€¢ 1 week before: Make tare and aroma oil (stores for months)
â€¢ 1-2 days before: Make broth, chashu, marinated eggs
â€¢ Day of: Slice toppings, heat broth, cook noodles, assemble
BROTH NOTES:
â€¢ Chintan = clear, delicate, traditional
â€¢ Paitan = creamy, rich, emulsified from vigorous boiling
â€¢ Tonkotsu = pork bone paitan, most labor-intensive but iconic
â€¢ All broths freeze excellently for 6+ months
TARE NOTES:
â€¢ Tare provides ALL the salt - unseasoned broth is bland!
â€¢ Shoyu = soy sauce based, most versatile, classic
â€¢ Shio = salt based, lets broth flavor shine
â€¢ Miso = paste based, adds body and earthy complexity
â€¢ Typical ratio: 10% tare to broth by volume
NOODLE NOTES:
â€¢ Fresh ramen noodles must contain wheat + kansui (alkaline salts) for authentic springy texture and yellow color
â€¢ Sun Noodle brand (frozen section) is excellent and widely available
â€¢ Cook in unsalted water - the tare seasons everything
â€¢ Fresh noodles actually improve after 1-2 days rest in fridge
COMPONENT FLEXIBILITY:
â€¢ Light broth + rich tare = balanced
â€¢ Rich broth + light tare = broth-forward
â€¢ Experiment to find your perfect combination!
Source: Adapted from The Ramen_Lord Book of Ramen by Mike Satinover

recipes.title: Sanguette (French Blood Pancake)
steps: Peel 1 garlic clove. Rub the bread slice with it until the garlic is used up. Cut bread into small cubes and place in a shallow dish.
Peel and finely chop the onion, shallot, and remaining garlic clove. Chop the parsley.
Add the chopped vegetables and parsley to the bread cubes. Mix well.
Pour fresh chicken blood evenly over the bread mixture. Let soak and coagulate for a few minutes.
Heat duck fat in a heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-high heat.
Once fat is melted and very hot, slide the blood mixture into the pan.
Cook like a pancake for 6-7 minutes per side, seasoning each side generously with salt and pepper.
When cooked through, slide the sanguette onto a warm serving plate.
Deglaze the pan with red wine vinegar, scraping well with a spatula.
Pour the deglazed juices over the sanguette and serve immediately.
notes: Volume conversions: Fresh blood â‰ˆ 240g (1 cup), 1 slice bread â‰ˆ 50g, 2 garlic cloves â‰ˆ 10g, 1 onion â‰ˆ 150g, 1 shallot â‰ˆ 30g, 4 parsley sprigs â‰ˆ 8g, 1 tbsp duck fat â‰ˆ 14g, vinegar â‰ˆ 15g (1 tbsp)
Traditional French country dish from poultry-raising regions
Fresh chicken blood must be from freshly slaughtered chicken
Blood will coagulate when mixed with bread
Served as a savory pancake or galette

recipes.title: Shakshuka
steps: Peel and slice onions into half or quarter rings. Core peppers and cut into slices.
Heat olive oil in large shallow pan over medium heat. Add onions and cook 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add peppers and cook another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, parsley, half of the cilantro (8g), and thyme. Stir and cook a couple minutes until starting to brown.
Add ground beef and brown, breaking into pieces.
Add tomatoes and cook 15-20 minutes to allow flavors to blend. Season with salt and pepper.
Push four indentations in sauce with back of spoon. Crack one egg into each indentation.
Sprinkle feta chunks over top. Cover and cook eggs 5 minutes, basting whites with sauce halfway through. Whites should be just cooked, yolks still soft.
Sprinkle remaining cilantro over top and serve with bread.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 large onion â‰ˆ 200g, 2 red peppers â‰ˆ 300g, 3 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 42g, 2 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 10g, 2 tbsp parsley â‰ˆ 8g, 4 tbsp cilantro â‰ˆ 16g, 1 tsp thyme â‰ˆ 3g
Can be made ahead up to step 5 and chilled or frozen

recipes.title: Shirazi Salad (Persian Cucumber Tomato Salad)

ingredients.name: white wine vinegar or sour grape juice

steps: Combine cucumber, tomato, and onion in a large bowl.
Sprinkle dried mint, salt, and pepper over vegetables.
Drizzle with olive oil, vinegar, and lime juice.
Toss everything together until evenly mixed.
Let the salad rest for 10-15 minutes if you have time to allow flavors to develop.
Serve and enjoy.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 cups cucumber â‰ˆ 300g, 2 cups tomatoes â‰ˆ 400g, Â½ cup onion â‰ˆ 75g, 3 tbsp olive oil â‰ˆ 42g, 2 tbsp lime juice â‰ˆ 30g, 2 tbsp vinegar â‰ˆ 30g, 1 tbsp dried mint â‰ˆ 4g
Fresh mint can be substituted for dried
No need to peel or deseed cucumbers - Persian, English, or regular varieties work
Vegetables can be chopped 3-4 days ahead; store undressed and add dressing before serving

recipes.title: Shuwa with Fragrant Rice
steps: Crush garlic and sea salt together using pestle and mortar into a smooth paste.
Blend in all dry spices, half of the lemon juice (15g), and half of the olive oil (50ml) until evenly combined.
Rub spice paste thoroughly over lamb. Cover and marinate overnight in refrigerator.
Preheat oven to 140Â°C (285Â°F). Remove lamb to reach room temperature.
Wrap lamb leg in banana leaves, seams facing down. Place in deep baking tray.
Cover with foil and bake 1-2 hours until tender with slight pink center.
Rest lamb covered for 30 minutes while cooking rice.
Soak saffron threads in 60ml warm water.
SautÃ© diced onion in remaining olive oil (80ml) until translucent.
Brown broken vermicelli pieces in the oil.
Add rice and coat with oil. Mix in soaked saffron, cinnamon, allspice, and ginger.
Add stock and bring to boil. Cover with lid and cook on low heat 20 minutes until liquid evaporates.
Fluff rice with fork and add remaining lemon juice (15g).
Carve rested lamb into slices and serve with rice.
notes: Volume conversions: Â½ garlic bulb â‰ˆ 25g, Â½ tsp salt â‰ˆ 3g, Â½ nutmeg grated â‰ˆ 2g, Â½ tbsp spices â‰ˆ 8g each, Â¼ tbsp turmeric â‰ˆ 4g, juice of Â½ lemon â‰ˆ 30g total, 50ml oil â‰ˆ 50g, 80ml oil â‰ˆ 80g
Traditional Omani dish
Can substitute aluminum foil for banana leaves if unavailable

recipes.title: Sinigang na Baboy (Pork Sinigang)

ingredients.name: pork spare ribs, cut into 2-inch pieces
bok choy or pechay, separated into leaves

steps: Rinse pork ribs and drain well.
In a pot over medium heat, combine pork with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, skimming scum from the surface.
Once broth clears, add tomatoes, onion, and fish sauce. Lower heat and simmer 1-1Â½ hours until meat is tender.
Add taro and cook 4-6 minutes until tender.
Add chili peppers and radish. Simmer 2-3 minutes.
Add long beans and cook 2 minutes.
Add eggplant and okra. Cook 1-2 minutes.
Add tamarind base powder and stir until dissolved. Season with salt and pepper.
Add bok choy and cook 1 minute.
Serve hot.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 lbs pork â‰ˆ 907g, 8 cups water â‰ˆ 1900g, 2 large tomatoes â‰ˆ 400g, 1 medium onion â‰ˆ 150g, 2 tbsp fish sauce â‰ˆ 30g, 1.5 packages tamarind powder â‰ˆ 40g
Fresh tamarind alternative: Boil 15 tamarind pods with 1 cup water until soft, mash and strain
Maintain approximately 8 cups liquid throughout cooking

recipes.title: Sizzling Sisig
steps: Combine pig face, vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, peppercorns, bay leaves, salt, and enough water to cover the pork in a large pot.
Bring to a boil, skimming scum from surface. Lower heat, cover, and simmer 50 minutes to 1 hour until tender.
Drain well and discard liquid.
Over hot grill, cook pork 7-10 minutes per side until crispy and charred. Alternatively, broil at 450Â°F (230Â°C) for 4-5 minutes until charred.
Cool slightly and chop meat into small pieces.
Mix chopped meat with onions, chili peppers, calamansi juice, and liquid seasoning.
Add liver spread and stir gently. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer to sizzling hot plates and serve.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 lbs pig face â‰ˆ 907g, 1 cup vinegar â‰ˆ 240g, Â¼ cup soy sauce â‰ˆ 60g, 1 head garlic â‰ˆ 45g, 1 tsp peppercorns â‰ˆ 3g, 1 tbsp salt â‰ˆ 15g, 1 large onion â‰ˆ 200g, 6 Thai chilies â‰ˆ 15g, Â½ cup calamansi juice â‰ˆ 120g, 1 tbsp liquid seasoning â‰ˆ 15g, Â½ cup liver spread â‰ˆ 120g
Originates from Pampanga, Philippines
Works as either an appetizer or main entrÃ©e
Can be served on regular plates if sizzling plates unavailable

recipes.title: Smoked Za'atar Lamb Breast
steps: Trim most fat from the lamb breast. Flip to the bone side and make shallow cuts through silver skin between bones to prevent curling.
Combine sumac, oregano, coriander, cumin, allspice, and salt in a bowl.
Coat lamb with olive oil and apply seasoning mix evenly on all sides.
Preheat smoker to 250Â°F (121Â°C).
Place lamb meat-side up and smoke for 1 hour.
Flip lamb and smoke for 45 minutes. Spritz with apple cider.
Return lamb meat-side up and smoke for 45 minutes. Spritz with apple cider every 30 minutes.
Wrap lamb in heavy-duty foil and smoke for 30 more minutes.
Rest at least 10 minutes before slicing.
Top with sesame seeds and lemon juice before serving.
notes: Volume conversions: 2 tsp sumac â‰ˆ 6g, 1 tsp oregano â‰ˆ 3g, Â½ tsp spices â‰ˆ 2g each, 1Â½ tsp salt â‰ˆ 7g, Â¼ tsp sesame seeds â‰ˆ 2g
Serve with couscous or tabbouleh for complementary sides
This cut is very fatty and forgiving - difficult to overcook

recipes.title: Sous Vide Cheesecake

ingredients.name: crumbled graham crackers or digestive biscuits

steps: Heat water to 176Â°F (80Â°C) using a sous vide machine. Make sure you have enough water in your cooking vessel to completely cover the jars.
Beat the cream cheese and sugar together in a large mixing bowl with a hand mixer at medium speed until fluffy and light.
Add the eggs, one at a time. Beat until the mixture is well combined.

Add sour cream, vanilla extract, salt, and lemon juice. Continue mixing until the cheesecake mixture reaches an even consistency without any lumps. Stop the mixer from time to time and scrape down the sides of the bowl to make sure everything is evenly incorporated.

Lay out 9 4-ounce jars. Scoop the cheesecake mixture into the jars leaving 1Â½ inches of room at the top of each jar. Tap the bottom of the jars a few times against a flat surface to shake out the air as much as possible from the mixture.
Seal the jars tightly with your fingers only. Do not use the strength of your full hand to tighten the lids. Air needs to come out from the jars while sous vide cooking.
Carefully lower the jars in the sous vide bath using tongs. Cook for 90 minutes.

Once the timer is up, remove the jars carefully and allow them to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate the cheesecake for at least 4 hours to let it set completely before serving (ideally overnight).

Make the crust: Place the graham crackers or digestive crackers in a zip-top bag and use a rolling pin to crumble them. In a medium skillet, melt butter over medium heat, add the crumbled crackers in the skillet to combine with the butter. Sprinkle with cinnamon. Transfer to a bowl and set aside to cool.

Optional - make the blueberry topping: In the same skillet or a small saucepan, cook the blueberries with sugar and lemon juice over low heat. Bring to a slow simmer. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stir gently and occasionally, until the blueberries just start to break down and the mixture is thickened. Remove from heat and allow to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until ready to use.
Serve: Open a jar of cheesecake, add a generous spoonful of blueberry topping and a big spoonful of the loosened crust on top.
notes: Volume conversions: 16 oz cream cheese â‰ˆ 454g, 1/3 cup sugar â‰ˆ 67g, 2 eggs â‰ˆ 100g, 1/2 cup sour cream â‰ˆ 120g, 1 tsp vanilla â‰ˆ 5g, 1/2 tsp salt â‰ˆ 3g, 1 tsp lemon juice â‰ˆ 5g, 1 tbsp butter â‰ˆ 14g, 1 cup graham crackers â‰ˆ 100g, 1/2 tsp cinnamon â‰ˆ 1g, 2 cups blueberries â‰ˆ 300g, 2 tbsp sugar â‰ˆ 25g, 1 tbsp lemon juice â‰ˆ 15g
Makes 9 individual 4-oz servings in jars
Can be made ahead - refrigerate for up to 5 days
Sous vide method ensures no cracks and perfectly smooth texture

recipes.title: Tom Kha Gai (Thai Coconut Chicken Soup)

ingredients.name: lemongrass stalk, pounded and cut into 2-inch pieces

steps: Heat coconut oil in a medium pot over medium heat.
Add onion, garlic, jalapeÃ±o, galangal, lemongrass, and curry paste. Cook 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until onions soften.
Add chicken broth and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes uncovered.
Strain out aromatics and discard.
Add coconut cream, chicken, and mushrooms. Simmer until chicken cooks through.
Season with fish sauce, coconut sugar, and lime juice to taste.
Cook 2 minutes longer.
Ladle into bowls and top with green onions and cilantro.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tbsp coconut oil â‰ˆ 14g, Â½ onion â‰ˆ 100g, 2 cloves garlic â‰ˆ 10g, Â½ jalapeÃ±o â‰ˆ 25g, 3 slices galangal â‰ˆ 15g, 1 lemongrass stalk â‰ˆ 15g, 2 tsp curry paste â‰ˆ 10g, 4 cups broth â‰ˆ 960g, 4 cups coconut cream â‰ˆ 960g
Can substitute 1-3 Thai chiles for jalapeÃ±o
Can use coconut milk instead of coconut cream
Whole30, paleo, and vegan adaptations available

recipes.title: Watermelon Gazpacho

ingredients.name: kosher salt, plus more to taste
extra-virgin olive oil (use the best you can find)
crema (or Greek yogurt, sour cream, or buttermilk)

steps: In a large bowl, toss together watermelon, tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, almonds, kosher salt, and pepper. Set aside to marinate for about 1 hour.
In a blender, working in batches, purÃ©e vegetables and their liquid until smooth and creamy.
Transfer soup to a large bowl. Whisk in sherry vinegar and olive oil. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
Cover soup and chill in refrigerator for at least 2 hours or up to 3 days.
When ready to serve, adjust seasoning again with salt and pepper to taste.
Combine crema and chopped Calabrian chiles in a small bowl.
Ladle chilled soup into bowls and garnish with the crema mixture. Optionally garnish with additional diced vegetables.
notes: Salting the vegetables in advance breaks down their cell walls, extracting more flavor.
Toasted almonds add texture and body without the heaviness of traditional bread.
Tangy crema and spicy Calabrian chiles balance the sweetness of the watermelon.
Crema's lighter body makes it easy to drizzle - Greek yogurt, sour cream, or buttermilk also work.

recipes.title: Yeast Bread
steps: In a large bowl, mix yeast, sugar, salt, and warm water. Let stand until yeast dissolves.
Gradually add flour one cup at a time, mixing thoroughly until dough pulls away from sides of bowl.

Turn dough onto a floured surface. Fold the far edge back over itself towards you. Press with heels of your hands and push away. Rotate 90Â° after each push. Repeat for 5 minutes, sprinkling only enough flour to prevent sticking.
Let dough rest. Scrape out and grease the mixing bowl with olive oil or non-stick spray.
Knead dough again for 2-3 minutes.
Return dough to bowl and turn over once to grease the top. Cover with damp towel and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1-2 hours.
Punch down dough and briefly knead out air bubbles. Cut in half and shape into two Italian or French-style loaves.
Place loaves on a cookie sheet generously sprinkled with cornmeal. Let rest 5 minutes.
Bring 4 cups (960g) water to a boil in a teakettle or pot.
Slash the tops of loaves 3 or more times diagonally. Brush lightly with cold water.
Place an aluminum roasting pan on the bottom of a cold oven. Fill pan with 1 inch of boiling water.
Slide loaves onto baking stone in the cold oven.
Bake at 400Â°F (205Â°C) for 35-45 minutes until golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped.
Cool for at least 1 hour before slicing with a sharp serrated bread knife.
notes: Volume conversions: 1 tbsp yeast â‰ˆ 9g, 1 tbsp sugar â‰ˆ 12g, 1 tbsp salt â‰ˆ 15g, 2 cups water â‰ˆ 480g, 5Â½-6 cups flour â‰ˆ 690g, 4 cups boiling water â‰ˆ 960g
Makes 2 loaves - can freeze one for later

Alternate crustier method: Let shaped loaves rise 45 minutes. Preheat oven to 500Â°F with empty roasting pan for 15-30 minutes. Brush loaves with water, place in oven, pour 1 cup boiling water into pan. Bake 10 minutes at 500Â°F, then 10 minutes at 400Â°F. Cool 30 minutes before slicing.
If no baking stone, use inverted cookie sheet (crust will be less crisp)
